item.title: US Companies Add 62,000 Jobs, Led by Health Care, ADP Says
item.description: US companies added more jobs than expected last month, suggesting the labor market may be stabilizing.

item.title: Record Net Bullish Bets on European Gas Add to Price Swings
item.description: Funds and other speculators boosted their net long positions in European natural gas to a record high before this week’s selloff in energy markets.

item.title: Chile Central Bank Says Some Members Floated Rate Hike in March
item.description: Chile’s central bank said on Wednesday that several board members put forward the idea of a quarter-point interest rate hike at their last policy decision, as the Middle East war stokes inflation worries.

item.title: South Africa Tax Collection Beats Estimate Despite Tough Climate
item.description: South Africa’s tax collection modestly beat estimates, boosted by stronger contributions from companies, personal income receipts and value-added tax, preliminary data show.

item.title: Commodity Windfalls Are Rolling Into Russia From War in Iran
item.description: The Iran war has generated billions of dollars of additional oil revenues for Russia, but Moscow’s windfall extends well beyond crude to gas, grain, aluminum and fertilizers.

item.title: US Premarket Movers: MSC, NCino, Nike, RH, Target Hospitality
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures rise 0.8% as of 7:46 a.m. in New York amid hopes that the war in Iran will end soon. Trump is set to address the nation with a prime time update at 9 p.m. Washington time.

item.title: S&P 500’s Weakest Performer Struggles to Escape Selling ‘Vortex’
item.description: Trade Desk Inc. investors who stuck with the stock through a 15-month selloff that has left it down more than 80% will likely be waiting awhile for a rebound — if one ever materializes.

item.title: US Stock Futures Rise as US, Iran Seek Exit From Iran War
item.description: US stocks rose in premarket trading on Wednesday as traders became more optimistic about US military operations in Iran ending soon.

item.title: Gasoline at $4 Alarms US Consumers and Raises Pressure on Trump
item.description: The president talks of ending the Iran war as key price milestone is surpassed.

item.title: Takaichi’s New BOJ Pick Avoids Policy Signals, Leans on Data
item.description: The Bank of Japan’s newly appointed board member Toichiro Asada played it safe at his first press briefing by indicating he would focus on data rather than emphasizing his existing views on monetary policy.

item.title: Watch Stocks, Not Pump Prices, to Gauge US Consumer Resilience
item.description: Surging equities have been supporting confidence. March’s 5% slump in the S&P 500 will hurt.

item.title: Banning Kids From Social Media Is Easier Said Than Done
item.description: Europe lacks a unified approach to another complex tech issue

item.title: Asian Factories Show Mixed Activity Amid Strains From Fuel Price Shock
item.description: Trump says war should end in a couple weeks as fuel costs soar

item.title: US Mortgage Rates March Higher to Seven-Month High of 6.57%
item.description: US mortgage rates climbed for a fourth straight week, rising to the highest since August and denting refinancing and home purchase activity.

item.title: NASA Set to Launch Astronauts to Moon for First Time in 50 years
item.description: NASA astronauts are poised to lift off Wednesday on a 10-day journey that will slingshot them around the moon, marking humanity’s return to the lunar vicinity for the first time in more than half a century.

item.title: Saudi Oil Exports Fell by 50% in March on Iran’s Hormuz Shutdown
item.description: Saudi Arabia’s crude oil exports fell by half in March after Iran effectively prevented tankers from leaving the Persian Gulf, forcing the kingdom to reroute flows to its west coast.

item.title: BYD Exports Jump On Iran Oil Shock as Domestic Slump Persists
item.description: BYD Co.’s exports and overseas sales climbed 65% in March as surging oil prices due to the Iran war boosted demand for electric cars, though the automaker continues to struggle to regain momentum in the China market.

item.title: Lingering War Risks Keep Investor Euphoria in Check
item.description: Higher-for-longer oil prices threaten economy, earnings.

item.title: Africa’s Biggest Fund Manager Seeks Buyer for Chicken Business
item.description: Africa’s biggest fund manager is seeking a buyer for Daybreak Foods (Pty) Ltd., its wholly owned chicken business in South Africa that has been plagued by scandals and a business-rescue process.

item.title: US Treasuries Extend Gains Before Retail, Manufacturing Data
item.description: US Treasuries climbed on speculation that a possible end to the war in Iran may pave the way for interest rate cuts, with upcoming economic data potentially offering clues on when the Federal Reserve may ease policy.

item.title: Ghana Inflation Cools Again Despite Oil-Price Hit From Iran War
item.description: Ghana’s annual inflation rate cooled, extending an easing streak to a 15th month despite pressure for higher fuel prices because of fallout from the Iran war.

item.title: Wall Street Traders Say Positioning Not Peace Is Driving Stocks
item.description: Wall Street’s largest trading desks say the extremely bearish positioning in equity markets going into the end of the quarter was the main driver of Tuesday’s big rebound in US stocks, rather than a shift in investor sentiment over the Iran war.

item.title: Some French Gas Stations Run Dry as Price Caps Spur Rush to Fill
item.description: Hundreds of French filling stations have run dry as drivers rushed to fill up vehicles, after fuel-price caps were introduced because of global supply disruptions caused by the Middle East war.

item.title: ECB’s Makhlouf Says Prolonged War Brings Adverse Scenario Closer
item.description: European Central Bank Governing Council member Gabriel Makhlouf said a lengthy war in the Middle East would steer the euro-area economy toward a worse outcome than the baseline laid out last month by policymakers.

item.title: EU Proposes Carbon Market Concessions to Tame Energy Prices
item.description: The European Union proposed an adjustment to its carbon trading program to limit the impact of emissions costs on soaring energy bills, while stopping short of immediately releasing additional volumes into the market.

item.title: Google Faces Demands to Prohibit AI Videos for Kids on YouTube
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google is facing demands from child development experts to prohibit videos created with artificial intelligence from being shown or recommended to young viewers across YouTube and YouTube Kids.

item.title: The AI Video Apps Gaining Ground After OpenAI Declared Sora Dead
item.description: Rival tools such as Kling AI, RunwayML and Vidu have all seen user gains in the week since OpenAI said it would shutter its Sora AI video generator.

item.title: Fed Faces War-Driven Inflation With Credibility Already Frayed
item.description: The next bout of US inflation looks set to kick in before the Federal Reserve is quite done cleaning up after the last one.

item.title: US Government Requests for Social Media User Data Are Soaring
item.description: US government requests for user data from technology companies has skyrocketed by 770% in the past decade, a new research report has concluded.

item.title: Rightmove Faces £1.5 Billion UK Lawsuit Over ‘Dominant’ Position
item.description: Rightmove Plc is facing a £1.5 billion ($2 billion) class action lawsuit over allegations that it abused its dominant position in the UK online property listings market, sending shares down the most in almost five months.
